response.justification: The candidate brings 17+ years of continuous software engineering across diverse domains and meets the tenure floor, but falls well short of the role's core requirements. The Principal Engineer / AI Strategy position demands deep, demonstrable AI expertise and director-level IC strategic influence — the candidate's AI work is confined to the current role (started April 2025), with no evidence of AI product ownership at scale, strategic customer or partner advisory, cloud architecture leadership, or external recognition in the AI/ML space. The overall profile reflects a strong hands-on senior/staff IC rather than the strategic, externally-facing AI leader the role requires.
strengths: 17+ years of uninterrupted software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure, and tooling — satisfies the minimum tenure requirement
Current role includes real AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks), signaling an active pivot toward the relevant domain
Entrepreneurial track record via GISCollective and multiple open-source projects, plus workshops and conference talks demonstrating a knowledge-sharing disposition
concerns: AI expertise is very recent (April 2025 onward) and narrowly scoped to tooling; no demonstrated AI products or platforms at a scale consistent with a director-level AI strategy mandate
No evidence of strategic customer or partner advisory work, roadmap influence, or industry-level thought leadership in AI or cloud
No advanced degree in AI or a related field; no publications, patents, or external recognition that would support a 'recognized expertise in AI' claim
